name: Tony Pollard
headline: Reliable bell-cow volume, dependable mid-range RB2
body:
Pollard delivered a quietly strong 2025, playing all 17 games on a 62.5 percent snap share with 242 carries for 1,082 yards (4.47 YPC) and five scores, plus 33 catches for 206 yards, totaling 152.8 fantasy points and an RB19 finish. The efficiency ticked up from 2024 (4.15 YPC, RB20) and his explosive-run rate held at 11.16 percent with a 65-yard touchdown the highlight. He broke 16 tackles (one every 15.1 carries) and added 1.83 yards after contact, durable workhorse traits that anchor his weekly floor in Tennessee.

The caveats are real: a -0.118 EPA per play, four fumbles, and a receiving role that has shrunk to a 7.7 percent target share with zero receiving touchdowns and a meager 0.59 yards per route. With Tyjae Spears lurking and Cam Ward still developing, Pollard is a volume-dependent RB2 whose ceiling is capped without goal-line dominance or pass-game work. Draft him as a stable flex-to-RB2 starter, not a league-winner.
